title: Squares of a Sorted Array
description:
Given an integer array `nums` sorted in non-decreasing order, return an array
of the **squares of each number**, also sorted in non-decreasing order.
constraints: nums is sorted in non-decreasing order

solutions.summary: The largest squares come from the extremes; fill the result back-to-front.

steps.title: Allocate the result and two pointers
steps.explanation: Make a result array of the same length and put `lo`/`hi` at the two ends.

code.python:
def sorted_squares(nums):
    n = len(nums)
    res = [0] * n
    lo, hi = 0, n - 1
    return res

steps.title: Fill from the back
steps.explanation: Walk an output index `k` from the end toward the front — that is where the next-largest square goes.

code.python:
def sorted_squares(nums):
    n = len(nums)
    res = [0] * n
    lo, hi = 0, n - 1
    for k in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
        pass
    return res

steps.title: Take the bigger end each time
steps.explanation: Compare absolute values; the larger one squares bigger, so place it at `k` and step that pointer inward.
